evals.name: obvious-split-refactor-plus-bugfix
evals.expected_output: Recommendation: split into 2 commits. Commit A = the validation refactor (request-validator.ts + the 3 API files). Commit B = the currency bugfix (format-currency.ts). Rationale should mention that the bugfix is an independent concern and should be revertable on its own.

evals.name: obvious-single-rename
evals.expected_output: Recommendation: single commit. Rationale should explain that the call-site changes are meaningless without the rename — splitting would create broken intermediate commits where the rename existed but call sites still used the old name.

evals.name: ambiguous-feature-plus-refactor
evals.expected_output: Judgment call — both answers are defensible. Good response will reason about whether the NotificationProvider extraction stands on its own merits (clearer code regardless of SMS) or only makes sense as scaffolding for SMS. If the refactor stands alone, recommend splitting into refactor + feature commits. If the refactor only exists to enable SMS, recommend a single commit telling the whole story. The reasoning matters more than the verdict.

evals.name: over-split-trap-feature-many-files
evals.expected_output: Recommendation: single commit. All six files serve one purpose: 'add the phoneNumber field to user signup'. A naive recommendation might say split into migration / types / API / UI / tests — the skill should resist this and explain that splitting per-layer creates 5 broken intermediate commits (migration without code that uses it, type without DB column, etc.) and serves nobody. This is the anti-fragmentation guard in action.
